Recommendation
We recommended the following:
The Municipal Accountant conduct analysis of the Due to LGUs account and prepare the necessary adjusting entries to correct errors and properly classify accounts;
The Municipal Accountant locate and send confirmation letters to identifiable listed government agencies in the Due to LGUs and Due to NGAs to validate if said amounts are due to them by the Municipality. Require said government agencies to execute duly notarized affidavits on the amounts due to them and/or to submit necessary documents; and
Determine and identify, based on available records and thru confirmation the source agencies of the subject unutilized balances and process immediately the return of the unutilized balances of fund transfers to concerned source agencies and/or to the National Treasury, as applicable.

Recommendation
We recommended the following to the Municipal Accountant:
Ensure that recording of expenditures under the Other MOE account be made only if the transaction cannot be appropriately classified within any of the specific object of expenditures in the MOOE classification; and
Strengthen the review and validation process of financial transactions to ensure accurate reporting, proper classification and correct recording of expenditures in their designated accounts.

Recommendation
We recommended, and the Municipal Budget Officer and the Municipal Accountant agreed to reconcile their records to ensure transparency and accurate reporting of the SAAOB and SCBAA and submit to the Audit Team the corrected or revised report.

Recommendation
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant:
Compare amounts in the trial balance, general ledger and financial statements to identify discrepancies;
Follow the trail of transactions from the general ledger to the trial balance and then to the financial statements to pinpoint the source of the error; and
Establish internal controls to prevent future errors such as using accounting software, double-checking entries.

Recommendation
We recommended the following to the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant:
Review and reconcile records in order to provide an accurate data in the financial statements. Provide appropriate disclosure in the Notes to FS to inform users of information of any discrepancy that cannot be reconciled yet as of reporting date; and
Determine the personnel accountable to the undeposited collection and issue demand letter, as necessary. Pending recovery of said amount, record the undeposited collection as receivable under his/her accountability.

Recommendation
We recommended, and Management agreed to the following:
The Municipal Accountant, with the assistance of the Municipal Treasurer, identify the nature and status of dormant accounts, and send confirmations to the concerned agencies;
The Municipal Treasurer revert back the balances to the Bureau of Treasury, the Source Agency or the general fund, as the case may be; and
The Municipal Treasurer monitor the activity or inactivity of its depository accounts and exert all efforts to prevent current and savings accounts from becoming dormant.

Recommendation
We recommended, and Management agreed to the following:
Inform all department/office heads to refrain from referring to brand names in making request for the procurement of goods and ensure faithful adherence to the provisions of RA 9184 and its IRR in all procurement activities;
Disapprove PRs with reference to brand names or return the same to the requesting office for revision; and
The BAC and its Secretariat review all PRs submitted and return to the requesting unit if the same is not compliant to existing standards.

Recommendation
We recommended, and Management agreed, on a regular basis the following:
Provide the Office of the Auditor at the beginning of the year with a list of all on-going government PPAs and those that are to be implemented during the year in accordance with Item 2.1 of COA Circular No. 2013-004;
Inform the Office of the Auditor within 10 days after the award of the infrastructure project or before the start of the program/activity that the appropriate project signboards and/or public notices are already posted in accordance with Item 3.1 of COA Circular No. 2013-004; and
Prepare and maintain a monthly project monitoring report of the PPAs.

Recommendation
We recommended that the Municipal Budget Office provides for the statutory appropriation of not less than five per cent of the estimated revenue from regular sources.

Recommendation
We recommended that the members of the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Council (LDRRMC) to include as a separate caption in the LDRRMFIP the list of projects and activities to be charged under the unexpended LDRRMF of the previous years.

Recommendation
We recommended that the members of the LDRRMC re-evaluate the planned procurements in terms of its necessity or exigency in line with the implementation of the DRRM Plan to avoid charging unnecessary expenses to LDRRMF, the goal of which is not detrimental to the promotion of the objectives of RA 10121.

Recommendation
We recommended to management the following:
Provide specific timeline for the period of implementation of the PPAs; and
Include only PPAs that have been thoroughly planned and prepared to start the actual execution or implementation phase in the local development investment plan (LDIP) to ensure optimal utilization of the 20 per cent DF.

Recommendation
We recommended and the LSB agreed to submit quarterly and annual reports using the SEF Budget Accountability Form No. 1 (Annex B of DepEd-DBM-DILG JC No. 1, s. 2017) on SEF utilization, as prepared by the Local Accountant, to the DepEd CO, through appropriate channels, copy furnished the local sanggunian concerned and Regional Offices of the DBM and DILG, within the timelines.

Recommendation
We recommended, and the LSB agreed to ensure the posting of the SEF Utilization Reports at the website of the LGU and/or in at least three conspicuous public places and the same be made not later than the 20th day after the end of each quarter as required under DepEd-DBM-DILG JC No. 1, s. 2017 and DILG MC No. 2024-021.

Submit the Schedule of Dormant Accounts quarterly on or before the 15th day of the ensuing month;

Consider the preparation of a request for write-off of the Receivable accounts which were considered dormant pursuant to COA Circular No. 2023-008 within one year from the effectivity of the Circular otherwise the provision of COA Circular No. 2016-005 shall apply.

The Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction Management Officer (MDRRMO) before preparing the LDRRMFIP validate and accustomed as to the needs of its constituents in accordance with the four thematic areas or disaster risk reduction and management such as disaster prevention and mitigation, disaster preparedness, disaster response and disaster rehabilitation and recovery and shall review and update annually to include the activities to be funded from the unexpended QRF and DRRMF-MOOE of the previous years which were transferred to the Special Trust Fund. (par 5.1.15);

The Municipal Budget Officer prepares and maintain a separate Registry of Appropriations, Allotments and Obligations for the LDRRMF for QRF, MOOE and CO; and

The Report of Sources and Utilization of LDRRMF be prepared monthly and certified correct by the Municipal Accountant and submitted on or before the 15th day after the end of each month to the Auditor through the LDRRMC and Municipal Development Council.
observations.reason: Reiterated as Observation No. 9 in this report | Submitted reports for the CY 2017 were not in the prescribed format.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the submission of supporting documents and/or compliance of requirements as indicated to facilitate proper disposition of subject transaction documents and that vouchers and payrolls onwards be duly processed and properly documented before they will be approved and paid to facilitate proper recording in the books of accounts and avoid possible suspension and/or disallowance in audit.
observations.reason: Payment of BAC Honorarium for the CY 2017 were still made even with incomplete documentation.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the BAC furnish the Auditor a printed copy of the Procurement Monitoring Report to provide basis for monitoring the status of the procurement activities of the municipality.
observations.reason: Semestral Procurement Monitoring Report for the CY 2017 were not submitted within the prescribed period.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Treasurer will practice the actual recording of cash transactions in her cashbooks and Treasury records.  That is, collections and deposits should be recorded in the Report of Collections and Deposits (RCD) on the day they were collected and/or received from the respective collectors and on the day they were actually deposited in the depository bank, respectively.  If not deposited during the month, monthly balances should be shown in the respective Cashbook – Cash In Bank to reflect the correct cash balance as of end the month.
observations.reason: The Municipal Treasurer still did not comply with the audit recommendations.

The Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant still did not able to comply with the audit recommendations. They informed that the Accounting Department will maintain stock cards for supplies and materials for CY 2017 transactions.

The LGU still did not comply with the audit recommendations since there was no proper turn-over of documents from the previous inventory committee members who are no longer in the service. The Local Chief Executive order reconstituting the inventory committee for CY 2017.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the LGU should stop appropriating funds for the AICS Program under the Legislative Department, and for all other programs/projects/activities, for that matter, which are considered as enforcement of the LGU’s annual budget, “since this is the domain of the Executive” and that any provision of law that empowers the Legislative or any of its members “to play any role in the implementation or enforcement of the law violates the principle of separation of powers and is thus unconstitutional.” (Page 38, Supreme Court Decision on PDAF)
observations.reason: The LGU still did not comply with the audit recommendations, there were disbursements for financial assistance as donations to various Barangays for their programs/projects/activities under the Legislative Department, hence incorporated as one of the observations of this Report.

observations.recommendation:
Require the faithful adherence of the rules and regulations on the receipt, utilization and reporting of the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund of the LGU so as not to defeat the purpose of the law as provided in Section 2 (e) of RA 10121 to strengthen the capacity of LGUs “to build the disaster resilience of communities, and to institutionalize arrangements and measures for reducing disaster risks, including projected climate risks, and enhancing disaster preparedness and response capabilities at all levels.”

Realignment of funds to cover those expenses which exceeded their respective appropriations should be sanctioned by the Local Chief Executive to ensure propriety, validity and legality of transactions to ensure that the activities as planned will be implemented effectively, efficiently and economically.
observations.reason: The Utilization of the 70% Mitigation Fund for CY 2018 still did not fully comply with the requirements under existing DRRMF laws, rules and regulations.

observations.recommendation: Require the faithful compliance on the requirements of Section 272 of RA 7160 in the application of proceeds of the additional One Percent SEF Tax so as to serve the purpose for which the law was created and give benefits to the LGU’s constituents particularly the needs of the education sector.
observations.reason: The management informed that the budget of the SEF was prepared by the Local School Board to effectively allocate the basic purposes of the fund. Our post-audit of the   expenditures out of the SEF for CY 2017, showed that there were disbursements for non-priority expenditures, thus incorporated in Part II of this Report.

observations.recommendation:
Require that the Municipal Treasurer/Cashier/ Accountant should submit the transaction documents: Disbursement Vouchers, Payrolls, Official Receipts, JEVs for adjustments, liquidations, etc. and their corresponding supporting documents, within the reglementary period to ensure timely evaluation of same and that deficiencies and/or other observations will be also immediately corrected and/or adjusted.

That is, the personnel involved in the daily recording of transactions in the books of accounts shall turnover the receipts and the disbursement records with all paid vouchers and documents evidencing the transactions to the Auditor within ten (10) days from the date of receipt of said documents pursuant to COA Circular No. 95-006 dated May 18, 1995.
observations.reason: Reiterated in Part II of this Report. Further the management informed that an additional manpower will be provided to handle segregation and submission of the vouchers for CY 2017.

observations.recommendation: Require the faithful compliance with existing laws, rules and regulations on claims for travelling expenses to ascertain the legality, validity and propriety of the transactions and avoid suspension and/or disallowance in audit.
observations.reason: There are still claims with excess per diems claimed.

observations.recommendation: Require that management faithfully adhere to the provision of COA Circular 77-61 on the required marking of “For Official Use Only” on each side of the motor vehicles to ensure that fuel, oil and lubricants are expended efficiently, effectively and economically and that government motor transportation are utilized properly.
observations.reason: Some vehicles were already marked with the official seal/logo of the Municipality; but no official list was submitted for verification; annual physical inventory was not conducted by management

observations.recommendation: We recommend the submission of supporting documents or compliance of requirements as indicated and/or submit and explanation and/or justification why the condition happened to facilitate proper disposition of subject transaction documents and that vouchers onwards be duly processed and properly documented before they will be approved and paid to facilitate proper recording in the books of accounts and avoid possible suspension and/or disallowance in audit.

We recommend that the concerned officials and employees render an accounting or settlement of the cash advances. The Municipal Treasurer is required to withhold the salaries and other emoluments of the concerned officials and employees for their outstanding cash advances otherwise malversation charge will be filed against the concerned accountable person under Article 217 and under Article 218, both of the Revised Penal Code or criminal prosecution under Section 128 of PD 1445.
As one of the mechanisms to ensure the efficiency and effectiveness of control for cash transactions, we recommend that the Municipal Accountant shall prepare or indicate on the face of the voucher, for future cash advances, a certification that the previous cash advance has been liquidated and accounted for in the books before a new cash advance is processed and/or paid, in accordance with Section 4.1.2 of COA Circular No. 2012-004 dated November 28, 2012.

We recommend the management to faithfully comply with the existing laws, rules and regulations on the grant and liquidation of cash advances to ascertain the legality, validity and propriety of the cash transactions and avoid suspension and/or disallowance in audit.

We recommend that management should follow strictly the limitation in appropriating discretionary fund for the Office of the Local Chief Executive as provided for under Section 325 (h) of R.A. No. 7160.

We recommend that the Municipal Treasurer/Cashier/Accountant should submit the transaction documents: Disbursement Vouchers, Payrolls, Official Receipts, JEVs for adjustments, liquidations, etc. and their corresponding supporting documents, within the reglementary period to ensure timely evaluation of same and those deficiencies and/or other observations noted will be immediately corrected and/or adjusted.
The personnel involved in the daily recording of transactions in the books of accounts shall turnover the receipts and the disbursement records with all paid vouchers and documents evidencing the transactions to the Auditor within ten (10) days from the date of receipt of said documents pursuant to COA Circular No. 95-006 dated May 18, 1995.

We recommend strict compliance with the relevant provisions of law and rules governing the administration of LDRRMF for government resources to be assured of having been administered and used properly, regularly, and legally according to the purposes for which the fund was created. Furthermore, management shall ensure that all processes and documentations prescribed under other relevant laws and rules are duly complied with for disbursements to be valid, necessary and effective in consonance with the purposes sought to be attained through said undertakings.
We recommend that the BAC furnish the Auditor a printed copy of the Procurement Monitoring Report to provide basis for monitoring the status of the procurement activities of the municipality.

We recommend for the strict adherence to the prescribed documentations to ensure the validity and propriety of propriety of procurements made. Specifically, require the suppliers the submission of all other documents that would prove their legitimacy as authorized dealers including the genuineness as well as effectiveness of the procured items, in the process, ensure they are responsive to the health needs of the agency’s constituents.

We recommend that the Municipal Health Officer follow strictly the provisions of DOH A.O No. 163 and any other pertinent provisions to ensure that drugs and medicines requisitioned, administered and dispensed with were of good quality, essential, safe, and affordable to the constituents.

We recommend:
The Local School Board to:
Follow strictly Joint Circular (JC) No. 1, s. 2017 of the DepEd, DBM and DILG in the budget preparation of the SEF;
Submit Quarterly the SEF Budget Accountability Form No. 1 (Annex B) on SEF Utilization as prepared by the Local Accountant, to the DepEd CO, through appropriate channels, copy furnished the local sanggunian concerned and Regional Offices of the DBM and DILG.
The Municipal Budget Officer to approve Obligation Requests (ObRs) and the Local Chief Executive to approve the Disbursement Vouchers for SEF in accordance with the Joint Circular (JC) No. 1, s. 2017 of the DepEd, DBM and DILG.

We recommend that the Municipal Treasurer remit in full the total amount of premium contributions to the GSIS withheld every month. Also, prioritize payment of mandatory expenditures such as those due to GSIS to avoid payment of penalties and charges.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer deposit the subject cash on hand and faithfully comply with existing laws, rules and regulations in the collection and deposit of all income of the LGU to ensure that all monies received are fully accounted and properly recorded in the books of accounts and avoid possible loss and/or misuse of the scarce government funds.

The Municipal Mayor on the other hand (1) regularly monitor the deposits of these collections (2) ensure that the Municipal Treasurer strictly comply with the existing regulation. Otherwise, institute disciplinary action on the practice of not depositing intact all collections.

We recommended the Municipal Accountant to immediately record the reconciling items identified and verified to be proper like the net interest income from the bank deposits so as to show the correct cash in bank balance in the monthly trial balance in accordance with the generally accepted accounting principles.

We recommended that:

the Municipal Accountant and the concern officials limit  the granting of cash advances to those provided for in COA Circular No. 97-002 dated February 10, 1997 and Section 89 of Presidential Decree (P.D.) No. 1445; and 

the documentary requirements common to all cash advances except for travels are provided in COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated July 14, 2012, Prescribing the Revised Guidelines and Documentary Requirements for Common Government Transactions be adhered to.

We recommended the management to faithfully comply with the existing laws, rules and regulations on the grant and liquidation of cash advances to ascertain the legality, validity and propriety of the cash transactions and avoid suspension and/or disallowance in audit.

We recommended that management require the Disbursing Officer to completely attach the necessary supporting documents to her claim before submitting the same to the Accounting Office. On the other hand, the Municipal Accountant check the completeness of the supporting documents before signing the Box “A” of the Disbursement Voucher and refrain from processing Disbursement Vouchers with incomplete documents, should there be lacking supporting documents for the claim, the same shall be returned to the claimant.

We recommended that the concerned officials and employees render an accounting or settlement of the cash advances. The Municipal Treasurer is required to withhold the salaries and other emoluments of the concerned officials and employees for their outstanding cash advances otherwise malversation charge will be filed against the concerned accountable person under Article 217 and under Article 218, both of the Revised Penal Code or criminal prosecution under Section 128 of PD 1445.

As one of the mechanisms to ensure the efficiency and effectiveness of control for cash transactions, we recommend that the Municipal Accountant shall prepare or indicate on the face of the voucher, for future cash advances, a certification that the previous cash advance has been liquidated and accounted for in the books before a new cash advance is processed and/or paid, in accordance with Section 4.1.2 of COA Circular No. 2012-004 dated November 28, 2012.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer be required to adherestrictly to the provisions ofSection 26, Volume I, COA Circular No. 2002-003 dated June 20, 2002 for LGUs.

We recommend that a JEV be prepared, printed and attached to the DVs. We recommended the Municipal Treasurer to:

record all checks chronologically in a Check Register and to require the claimants or their duly authorized representative to acknowledge receipt of these checks by affixing their signature on the Check Register upon release.

prepare the RCI daily summarizing all check disbursements and submit the same to the Accounting Office together with the check disbursement vouchers.

We recommended the submission of supporting documents and/or compliance of requirements as indicated to facilitate proper disposition of subject transaction documents and that vouchers onwards be duly processed and approved before they will be paid to facilitate proper recording in the books of accounts and avoid possible suspension and/or disallowance in audit.

We recommended that the remittance of government share for employees contribution to HDMF beyond the maximum amount of contribution be stopped. The remittance of government share for employees contribution be in the amount of P100.00 per employee per month unless otherwise the Board of Trustees fixed a higher amount.

We recommended that Management refrain from granting additional allowances or other forms of compensation to any government official or employees specifically to MERT, except those specifically authorized by law.

We recommended that:

the Municipal Budget Officer to properly charge the transactions within the limitations of the budget and ensure that meals and snacks served during meetings, conferences and for entertainment of visitors and guests shall be properly charged against the representation allowance of the concerned LGU officials; and

the Municipal Accountant to check the propriety and validity of the claims.
observations.reason: The management minimized charging of meals and snacks to Representation Expenses that were not essential in the conduct of services. Decrease of amount were observed compared to prior years’ balances.

We recommended that:

the BAC upon receipt of the Purchase Request check and verify whether the items requested specify or with reference to brand names, if so, return the PR to the Office concerned for correction and modification; and

the BAC and the concerned officials strictly comply with the provisions of RA 9184 and its RIRR in all its procurement activities to prevent incurrence of illegal expenditures.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer/Cashier/Accountant should submit the transaction documents: Disbursement Vouchers, Payrolls, Official Receipts, JEVs for adjustments, liquidations, etc. and their corresponding supporting documents, within the reglementary period to ensure timely evaluation of same and those deficiencies and/or other observations noted will be immediately corrected and/or adjusted.

We recommend that a JEV be prepared, printed and attached to the DVs.  In the preparation of the report, the Accounting Unit shall prepare the JEVs in two copies and the columns be properly filled-up so that all items are properly identified and accounted for to ensure fairness of accounts presentation in the financial statements.

We recommended the Municipal Treasurer to:
record all checks chronologically in a Check Register and to require the claimants or their duly authorized representative to acknowledge receipt of these checks by affixing their signature on the Check Register upon release.
prepare the RCI daily summarizing all check disbursements and submit the same to the Accounting Office together with the check disbursement vouchers.

We recommended the submission of supporting documents and/or compliance of requirements as indicated to facilitate proper disposition of subject transaction documents and that vouchers onwards be duly processed and approved before they will be paid to facilitate proper recording in the books of accounts and avoid possible suspension and/or disallowance in audit.

We recommended that the remittance of government share for employees’ contribution to HDMF beyond the maximum amount of contribution be stopped. The remittance of government share for employees’ contribution is in the amount of P100.00 per employee per month unless otherwise the Board of Trustees fixed a higher amount.

We recommended that:
the Municipal Vice-Mayor before approving the disbursements of transfer of funds, adopt a system of internal control by evaluating the purposes of the programs and projects of the Barangay, see to it that copies of Program of Works and Approved Project Expenditures or Estimated Expenses indicating the project objective and expected output are submitted to determine the amount needed by the barangay and formulate a MOA/Trust Agreement stipulating thereto the manner of utilization and reporting and monitoring of such funds to ensure that government resources are not wasted or misused; and
the Municipal Accountant ensure that the disbursements for fund transfer be supported by the necessary documents as provided for in Section 3 of COA Circular No. 2012-001 to ensure the legality, validity and propriety of the subject claims.

We recommended that Management refrain from granting additional allowances or other forms of compensation to any government official or employees specifically to MERT, except those specifically authorized by law.

We recommended that the Municipal Budget Officer in approving Obligation Request (ObR) for obligations charged against LDRRMF ensure that the same is in accordance with the guidelines set under Paragraphs 5.1 to 5.4 of Section 5 of the Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-1 dated March 25, 2013 of the National Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Council, Department of Budget and Management and Department of the Interior and Local Government.
On the other hand, the Local Chief Executive approve only the procurement within the sample lists of the Memorandum Circular No. 2012-73 of the DILG such that the 70% of the 5% may be utilized only to procure early warning systems, preparedness equipment and other equipage and in relation to the corresponding activities under the four aspects such as: (a) prevention and mitigation (b) preparedness (c) response and (d) reconstruction and rehabilitation.
Also, the MDRRMO request a budget/appropriation in the General Fund proper necessary in the conduct of their day to day operations in their Office since the Circular clearly emphasizes the utilization of Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund for disaster reduction, preparedness and mitigation measures.

We recommended that:
The Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction Management Officer (MDRRMO) before preparing the LDRRMFIP validate and accustomed as to the needs of its constituents in accordance with the four thematic areas or disaster risk reduction and management such as disaster prevention and mitigation, disaster preparedness, disaster response and disaster rehabilitation and recovery and shall review and update annually to include the activities to be funded from the unexpended QRF and DRRMF-MOOE of the previous years which were transferred to the Special Trust Fund. (par 5.1.15);
The Municipal Budget Officer prepares and maintain a separate Registry of Appropriations, Allotments and Obligations for the LDRRMF for QRF, MOOE and CO; and
The Report of Sources and Utilization of LDRRMF be prepared monthly and certified correct by the Municipal Accountant and submitted on or before the 15th day after the end of each month to the Auditor through the LDRRMC and Municipal Development Council.

We recommended that:
the Municipal Budget Officer to properly charge the transactions within the limitations of the budget and ensure that meals and snacks served during meetings, conferences and for entertainment of visitors and guests shall be properly charged against the representation allowance of the concerned LGU officials; and
the Municipal Accountant to check the propriety and validity of the claims.

We recommended that:
the BAC upon receipt of the Purchase Request check and verify whether the items requested specify or with reference to brand names, if so, return the PR to the Office concerned for correction and modification; and
the BAC and the concerned officials strictly comply with the provisions of RA 9184 and its RIRR in all its procurement activities to prevent incurrence of illegal expenditures.

We recommended the submission of supporting documents and/or compliance of requirements as indicated to facilitate proper disposition of subject transaction documents and that vouchers and payrolls onwards be duly processed and properly documented before they will be approved and paid to facilitate proper recording in the books of accounts and avoid possible suspension and/or disallowance in audit.
We recommend that the BAC furnish the Auditor a printed copy of the Procurement Monitoring Report to provide basis for monitoring the status of the procurement activities of the municipality.

We recommended to limit the utilization of the Local Development Fund to the programs and projects enumerated under the DILG-DBM JMC No. 2011-1 dated April 13, 2011 and ensure strict monitoring of disbursements charged against such fund to attain desirable socio-economic development and environmental management outcomes.

We recommended that the Local School Board through the Municipal Budget Officer appropriate funds for projects/programs/ activities as mandated and authorized by law and as prioritized under provisions of RA 7160. The application and implementation of the programs be within what was allowed and determine expenses not to include any of those specifically enumerated as an exclusions from the SEF expenditures. All activities be aligned within the priorities as mandated to assure compliance as to its appropriation, authority, and payment.

Require the Municipal Treasurer to furnish the Auditor with copies of contracts, letter orders or purchase orders and notice of deliveries, as the case maybe, within five (5) days and twenty-four (24) hours, respectively, from such issuance and acceptance, in order to enable the Auditor to prepare for and perform timely review, evaluation and inspection of the same as required under existing laws, rules and regulations.

Require also the Municipal Engineer to submit request for inspection of completed projects to complete the technical evaluation process of subject infrastructure projects.
observations.reason: Although the Municipal Treasurer submitted copies of contracts, letter orders or purchase orders and notice of deliveries to our end for CY 2016 but still it is not within five (5) days and twenty-four (24) hours respectively, from such issuance and acceptance.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that management should adhere to the rules and regulations in the implementation and processing of AICS claims pursuant to Section 4(2) & (6) of P. D. 1445 and DSWD Administrative Order No. 5, Series of 2008 as amended by DSWD Administrative Order No.1, series of 2011, in order to ensure the legality, validity and propriety of such claims.

The Municipal Accountant is reminded of her responsibility of checking the completeness of the supporting documents before a transaction should be processed for payment to avoid suspension and/or disallowance in audit.
observations.reason: Some disbursements on financial assistance under the Aid to Individual in Crisis Situation (AICS) were not supported with the required documents thus subject for issuance of Notices of Suspension.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend the refund of honoraria claimed without legal basis; that subject honoraria granted to concerned personnel be stopped; and that the LGU will refrain from paying claims of this nature in the future to avoid disallowance in audit.

Management is enjoined to adhere to existing rules and regulations in the grant of honoraria to ensure the legality of transactions.  It is reminded that agency heads shall be held responsible and personally liable for honoraria payments not in accordance with the provisions of above-cited circular, without prejudice, however, to the refund of any excess payments by the personnel concerned. (Section 6.0, DBM Budget Circular No. 2007-2 dated October 1, 2007)
observations.reason: Honoraria to those concerned officials has already been stopped.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend the refund of honoraria claimed without legal basis; that subject honoraria granted to concern National Government Officials be stopped; and that the LGU will refrain from paying claims of this nature in the future to avoid disallowance in audit.

Management is enjoined to adhere to existing rules and regulations in the grant of honoraria to ensure the legality of transactions.  It is reminded that agency heads shall be held responsible and personally liable for honoraria payments not in accordance with the provisions of above-cited regulations, without prejudice, however, to the refund of any excess payments by the personnel concerned. (Section 6.0, DBM Budget Circular No. 2007-2 dated October 1, 2007)
observations.reason: Honoraria to those concerned officials has already been stopped.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend the refund of honoraria claimed without legal basis; that subject honoraria granted to concern members of the Citizen Crime Watch be stopped; and that the LGU will refrain from paying claims of this nature in the future to avoid disallowance in audit.

Management is enjoined to adhere to existing rules and regulations in the grant of honoraria to ensure the legality of transactions.  It is reminded that agency heads shall be held responsible and personally liable for honoraria payments not in accordance with the provisions of above-cited regulations, without prejudice, however, to the refund of any excess payments by the personnel concerned.
observations.reason: Honoraria to those concerned officials has already been stopped.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management should stop the granting of regular cash assistance to Citizen Crime Watch (CCW) for its regular operations; or for any NGO/PO for that matter; and should follow the proper procedures in the grant, release, utilization and recording of funds released to NGOs/POs as provided under existing laws, rules and regulations and refrain from paying claims of this nature in the future without complete supporting documents to avoid disallowance in audit.
observations.reason: Grants and Donations given to Citizen Crime Watch Organization has already been stopped.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that Management require the submission of dully filled-up drivers’ trip tickets to support the payment of gasoline, oil and lubricants totalling P7,808,135.61 to facilitate monitoring & preparation by the LGU of the required monthly reports relative to fuel consumption.

Moreover, we recommend that Management onwards strictly enforce complete and proper accomplishment of drivers’ trip tickets to support payments of gasoline, oil and lubricants to minimize wasteful, excessive and unnecessary expenditures for fuel consumption.
observations.reason: Drivers’ Trip Tickets supporting payments for gasoline, oil and lubricants were properly accomplished.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management to stop the practice of fuelling private vehicles out of government sources. Concerned officer or personnel can claim equivalent fare when using private vehicles for official travels with the corresponding certification. Also, strengthen controls in the withdrawal and consumption of fuel to ensure expenditures pertaining thereto are not excessive and unnecessary. Properly account for fuel consumption by ensuring that trip tickets and fuel consumption report are properly accomplished and approved by authorized officials.
observations.reason: For CY 2016, no private vehicle was allowed to claim fuel and gasoline.

observations.recommendation: Require the submission of supporting documents and/or compliance of requirements as indicated in the respective Audit Observation Memorandum (AOM) and/or Notice of Suspension (NS) issued to facilitate proper disposition of subject transaction documents and that vouchers and payrolls onwards be duly processed and properly documented before they will be approved and paid to facilitate proper recording in the books of accounts and avoid possible suspension and/or disallowance in audit.
observations.reason: The Management informed that they require settlement of those concerned officials and employees with issuance of Notice of Suspension. Still, some of the transactions for CY 2016 were subject for issuances of Notices of Suspension due to non-submission of the required supporting documents.

observations.recommendation: Require the faithful adherence of the provisions of RA 9184 particularly the action of the BAC taking into consideration that “The BAC shall be responsible for ensuring that the procuring entity abides by the standards set forth by the Act and this IRR, x x x.” (Section12.2, RA 9184)
observations.reason: For CY 2016, there was no payment of reimbursement of various goods and materials without sanction by the BAC.

observations.recommendation:
Require the faithful adherence of the rules and regulations on GAD funds so as not to defeat the purpose of the law so reiterated in PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-01 which is the appropriation of at least five percent (5%) of its total budget appropriation for the year for the development of programs, activities, and projects (P/A/P) that pursue women’s empowerment and gender equality; 

Realignment of funds to cover those expenses which exceeded their respective appropriations should be sanctioned by the Local Chief Executive to ensure propriety, validity and legality of transactions to ensure that the activities as planned will be implemented effectively, efficiently and economically.
observations.reason: The Management informed that their Annual GAD Plan is reviewed and approved by the DILG, but this was not submitted to our end prior to our verification on the disbursements of the fund.

observations.recommendation: Require the Municipal Treasurer to require all establishments to submit sworn statements of their gross income and revive the use of presumptive income level as required in the Revenue Code to determine the propriety of the business taxes to be imposed to them not to deprive the municipality of the additional income to be collected.
observations.reason: The submission of presumptive income was required to business establishments before issuing Business Permit. But these businessmen are not interested to submit their sworn statement.

observations.recommendation: Provisions cited on the tax ordinance should be strictly observed.  Require businessmen to submit sworn statements of their gross sales and Presumptive Income Level should be prepared before computing.
observations.reason: Monitoring were allegedly made by the Office of the Municipal Treasurer but no status reports were submitted to update the data/information from the taxpayers.

observations.recommendation: Enforce the remedies and measures prescribed under Sections 254 and 258 of RA 7160 to collect delinquent Real Property Taxes.  Collection system should be strengthened through intensified tax campaign to eliminate and/or minimize delinquencies.
observations.reason: Distribution of the Notices of Delinquency and Warranty of levy were allegedly made by the Municipal Treasurer to all barangays but no status reports were submitted to update the delinquent taxes.
